# AnubisX Framework — A Formal Framework for Behavioral Digital Attribution

## About
AnubisX is an original research framework that formalizes the problem of attributing digital artifacts to their human source through behavioral evidence. It provides a unified theoretical foundation, mathematical formalism, algorithmic catalog, and open-source prototype.

## Author
Ahmed Awad (NullC0d3) — Independent Researcher, Behavioral Digital Attribution
ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0005-0654-3393
Email: anubisxframework@gmail.com

## Key Facts
- Version: 3.0.0 (July 2026)
- 16 axioms in 6 groups
- 292 mathematical objects across 24 categories
- 37 algorithms across 5 behavioral modalities
- 6-layer architecture
- 31 validation criteria across 4 tiers
- License: CC BY 4.0
- DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21393392

## Behavioral Modalities
1. Stylometric — Vocabulary, syntax, discourse patterns (VALIDATED)
2. Chrono-Profiling — Circadian rhythms, temporal activity patterns (FUTURE)
3. Terminal Profiling — Command sequences, navigation patterns (FUTURE)
4. Network Analysis — Graph topology, community structure (FUTURE)
5. Media Forensics — File structure, metadata, naming conventions (FUTURE)

## Core Concepts
- Cognitive Centroid: Behavioral identity as an asymptotic attractor in high-dimensional feature space
- Behavioral Signal Model: y(s,t,c,p) = y_cog(s,t) + y_ctx(c) + y_plat(p) + ε(s,t,c,p)
- Likelihood Ratio Evidence Framework for attribution under uncertainty

## Cite As
Awad, A. (2026). AnubisX: A Formal Framework for Behavioral Digital Attribution (Version 3.0.0) [Computer software]. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21393392
